stem: Line p is defined by y = 5x + 2/9. Line q is perpendicular to line p in the xy-plane. What is the slope of line q?
rationale: Choice C is correct. The slope of line p is 5 (the coefficient of x in y = mx + b form). Line q is perpendicular to line p, so its slope is the opposite reciprocal of 5, which is -1/5. Choice A reverses the sign incorrectly. Choice B is the y-intercept of line p. Choice D is the slope of line p itself, which would make the lines parallel, not perpendicular.
